title: Quiz: Quantum Period Finding
description: Test your understanding of quantum period finding

questions.question: The unitary U_a for modular exponentiation acts as:
questions.explanation: U_a multiplies the state by a modulo N: |y⟩ → |ay mod N⟩.

questions.question: The eigenvalues of U_a are of the form:
questions.explanation: Eigenvalues are e^(2πis/r) for s = 0, 1, ..., r-1, where r is the period.

questions.question: After QPE on U_a, we measure a value that approximates:

options.text: A phase s/r for random s

questions.explanation: QPE measures phases. For U_a, we get s/r where s is uniformly random in {0,...,r-1}.

questions.question: Continued fractions are used to extract r from the measured phase s/r.
questions.explanation: The continued fraction algorithm efficiently finds the denominator r from an approximation to s/r.
